title: The Polynomial Hierarchy of Graph Problems
description: In COMP3023 and COMP4003, we briefly introduce the computational complexity classes P, NP, and NP-complete. However, there are many problems harder than NP but easier than PSPACE(the problems can be solved using polynomial memory space). To fill this gap, people define the polynomial hierarchy, which roots on NP as the ground level, and grows infinite levels to PSPACE as a fix-point. This project will study the complexity of some open problems and locate their levels in the polynomial hierarchy.

title: Game Design
description: For fun

title: Bring Your Own Idea
description: Any interesting idea is welcome.

title: High -Dimensional Gaussian Splatting for High -Fidelity Animatable Avatars
description: We study the high -dimensional Gaussian Splatting, which is a novel  extension of 3D Gaussian Splatting for high -quality animatable avatars. 3D  Gaussian Splatting represents a face through a collection of 3D Gaussian  primitives. 3DGS excels at rendering stat ic faces, but the state -of-the-art still  struggles with nonlinear deformations, complex lighting effects, and fine  details. We study how to extend higher dimensionality to increase  expressivity through conditioning on a learnable local embedding.

title: Whole -Body 3D Avatar with Pose -Driven Deformations from a Single Image
description: We study a reconstruction method that combines the strengths of 3D  Gaussian Splatting rendering and diffusion -based human animation method  to obtain a personalized 3D human avatar with pose -driven deformations  from a single image.

title: High -Fidelity Deblurring via 3D Gaussian Splatting
description: We study a reconstruction method that combines 3D Gaussian Splatting with  camera aperture modeling to obtain sharp 3D scenes from blurry multi -view  images.

title: High -Resolution Image Generation with Diffusion Models
description: We study a super -resolution method that combines latent -space alignment  and localized detail enhancement to obtain high -fidelity 1K+ images with  sharp textures from low -resolution inputs.

title: Learning to Prompt for Biomedical Vision -Language Models
description: Effectively adapting vision -language models (VLMs) to downstream  applications remains challenging, as their accuracy often depends on time - intensive and expertise -demanding prompt engineering, while full model  fine-tuning is costly. Explorations in prompt learning for biomedical image   analysis are still highly limited. In this work, we study a prompt learning  framework that enables efficient adaptation of BiomedCLIP for accurate and  highly generalizable few -shot biomedical image classification.

title: Synthetic financial time series data generation
description: Synthetic data has high value in a variety of fields including finance, because it can provide a large amount a data with low cost. In finance, it can be used as an alternative simulation model when the historical data is scarce.   In this project, you are expected to conduct a comprehensive literature review on the current methods of synthetic financial time series data generation, and develop or/and implement data generation method such as GANs, transformer; and evaluate your generating outcome.

title: Optimal investment, consumption problem in retirement management
description: In this project, it is expected to build a DNN-based decision support tools for individuals to optimize their spending and investment for better retirement life.

title: Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks (KANs) and its applications
description: In this project, you are expected to conduct a comprehensive study of the KANs models, investigate the architecture and evaluate their performance

title: Dynamic Integrated Climate-Economy (DICE) model and optimal climate policies
description: In this project, you are expected to build the DICE model incorporating climate and economic uncertainties, and optimize mitigation strategies under uncertainties, such as carbon prices and technology advancements. You will use Monte Carlo simulation for the random variables and DNN based regression method to solve

title: Dynamic Bayesian networks (DBN) applications: Dynamic Integrated Climate-Economy (DICE) model and optimal climate policies
description: A DBN naturally models a discrete-time, stochastic dice-rolling process with hidden state (which die is used) and observable outcomes (roll results). In this project, you are expected to represent DICE model as a Dynamics Bayesian networks, learn the parameters from data and conduct probabilistic inference, and use the posterior from inference to compute optimal climate polices. Dynamic Bayesian Networks provide a unified probabilistic framework to represent the DICE model’s dynamic climate-economy structure, perform state filtering and parameter learning, quantify deep uncertainty, and compute adaptive optimal climate policies that update as new climate and economic observations become available.

title: Human Activity Recognition with Large Language Models
description: In smart city applications, we have leveraged large language models (LLMs) to process radio frequency (RF) data efficiently. We expect LLMs can be extended to analyze data collected from other sensors (together with RF signals) in IoT devices to enable different applications. For instance, there are large amounts of data collected from inertial measurement units (IMUs) embedded in IoT devices, which include an accelerometer and a gyroscope. These data are crucial in many applications, such as human activity recognition (HAR). We can tokenize and structure the sequential sensor data so that LLM techniques can be successively applied.  The project team will study state-of-the-art works on HAR, re-implement corresponding schemes, and design an HAR algorithm based on insight observations.

title: Human Activity Recognition on Edge Devices
description: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) on edge devices is a key research direction in wearable computing and IoT, enabling real-time, privacy-preserving activity monitoring without cloud reliance. Unlike cloud-centric systems, prone to data leakage and high latency, edge-based HAR deploys lightweight models on resource-constrained devices, which collect wireless, accelerometer, or gyroscope data to classify activities like walking, running, and sitting. A core challenge is balancing model performance and efficiency: deep learning models must be compressed to fit edge devices’ limited memory and power, while maintaining accuracy. Despite this, edge-based HAR offers advantages including reduced bandwidth, enhanced privacy, and real-world applicability (e.g., elderly care, fitness tracking).   The project team will study state-of-the-art works on HAR, re-implement corresponding schemes, and design an HAR algorithm based on insight observations.

title: Negative Sampling in Graph Neural Networks
description: Negative sampling is a widely used and effective strategy in graph neural networks (GNNs) for alleviating the computational burden of full-graph training and improving discriminative representation learning. Instead of using all non-connected node pairs as negative examples, which is computationally prohibitive on large-scale graphs, negative sampling constructs a manageable set of false pairs by randomly selecting non-adjacent nodes. This strategy not only reduces the complexity of contrastive learning and link prediction objectives but also helps the model distinguish between real structural dependencies and spurious correlations. By incorporating carefully sampled negative instances during training, GNNs can learn more robust and generalizable node embeddings, thereby enhancing performance on downstream tasks such as node classification, link prediction, and graph recommendation.  The project team will study state-of-the-art works on negative sampling, re-implement corresponding schemes, and design an algorithm based on insight observations.

title: Graph Condensation
description: Graph condensation aims to synthesize a small, highly informative synthetic graph from a large-scale original graph, such that a graph neural network trained on the condensed graph can achieve performance close to that obtained by training on the full real graph. By reducing the number of nodes and edges while preserving critical structural and attribute information, graph condensation effectively alleviates the computational and memory challenges of training GNNs on massive real-world graphs. Existing methods typically optimize the node features and topology of the synthetic graph through bilevel optimization, knowledge distillation, or contrastive matching, capturing the essential distribution and relational patterns that dominate downstream tasks such as node classification and link prediction. This paradigm enables efficient model training, fast prototyping, and scalable deployment of GNNs in resource-constrained environments, making it particularly valuable for large-scale graph learning systems.  The project team will study state-of-the-art works on graph condensation, re-implement corresponding schemes, and design an algorithm based on insight observations.

title: 3D Reconstruction with Wireless Signals
description: 3D reconstruction with wireless signals (e.g., RF signals, Wi-Fi, or millimeter-wave) has emerged as an innovative and contact-free approach for reconstructing physical environments and objects, overcoming the limitations of traditional vision-based methods in low-light, occluded, or visually degraded scenarios. By leveraging the propagation characteristics of wireless signals—such as reflection, diffraction, and time-of-flight—this technique extracts spatial information from signal measurements (e.g., received signal strength, phase, or channel state information) to reconstruct 3D structures. A core advantage is its ability to penetrate non-metallic obstacles, enabling reconstruction in environments where optical sensors fail. Key challenges include mitigating signal noise, handling multipath effects, and converting noisy signal data into accurate 3D coordinates. Recent advances in signal processing, machine learning (e.g., cGANs, deep feature extraction), and wireless hardware have enhanced the precision and efficiency of this approach, expanding its applications in smart homes, autonomous navigation, security monitoring, and remote sensing.  The project team will study state-of-the-art works on wireless 3D reconstruction, re-implement corresponding schemes, and do the analysis based on insight observations.

title: Improving Inference Efficiency of Reasoning-Capable LLMs
description: Reasoning-capable large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into mainstream AI services, such as ChatGPT, DeepSeek, and Qwen, due to their strong ability to handle complex tasks requiring multi-step reasoning. We provide several representative use cases below. When interacting with systems like ChatGPT, users often pose queries that require step-by-step logical reasoning to produce a final answer, such as solving mathematical problems, generating code, or organizing structured plans. To address such queries, reasoning LLMs generate intermediate thinking steps (i.e., reasoning tokens) before arriving at the final answer, which typically leads to superior performance compared to non-reasoning LLMs. However, this process substantially increases the number of generated tokens, resulting in higher computational overhead and latency. Compared to standard LLMs, reasoning LLMs are therefore more expensive to run, less scalable, and more difficult to deploy in real-time or resource-constrained environments. As a result, improving the inference efficiency of reasoning-capable LLMs is of critical importance.  The project team will study state-of-the-art works on reasoning models, re-implement corresponding schemes, and do the analysis based on insight observations.

title: Geolocation -based game
description: People love games but it will be physically unhealthy if they keep you indoor and  stationary, so, let’s get moving with geolocation -based games!     Geolocation -based mobile game  is a type of mobile game where the gameplay  evolves and progresses v ia a player's location in the real world, often attained using  GPS. Most location -based games are mobile games that make use of the mobile  phone’s built in GPS capability, and often have real -world map integration.     In this project, you will implement a ge olocation -based game of your own design. The  game runs on mobile devices and can be single or multiple player, with or without  augmented reality.     Below is a list of notable examples:   • Pokémon Go   • Harry Potter: Wizards Unite  (discontinued)   • Ingress   • Geocaching   • BotFighters[18]  • Dragon Quest Walk   • Shadow Cities  (discontinued)   • Maguss  (discontinued)   • Minecraft Earth  (discontinued)   • Cluetivity

title: Application Based on Game Engines
description: A game engine is a software framework primarily designed for the development of  video games and generally includes relevant libraries and support programs. In many  cases, game engines provide a suite of visual development tools in addition to  reusable soft ware components. These tools are generally provided in an integrated  development environment to enable simplified, rapid development of applications in  a data -driven manner.     Unity and the Unreal Engine are currently the two most popular choices for  devel opers. A lot of games are built on top of these two, including Pokémon Go,  Monument Valley, Call of Duty: Mobile, Hogwarts Legacy, and PUBG: Battlegrounds.     In this project, you will study a game engine and build a 2D/3D application with a topic  of your ow n choice. This project may require 3D modeling and 3D animation  technology, too.

title: Academic Collaboration Networks  on DBLP
description: DBLP (Digital Bibliography & Library Project) is a comprehensive database of  computer science publications, providing metadata such as authors, titles, journals,  and citation relationships. Data analysis based on DBLP involves extracting insigh ts  from this rich dataset to understand academic trends, collaboration patterns, and  research impact.   One possible direction in this domain  is Academic Collaboration Networks  (ACNs) .  CAN s are structured systems that map and analyze interactions among resea rchers,  institutions, or disciplines through shared publications, co -authorships, and joint  projects. These networks leverage network science and bibliometric analysis to  uncover patterns in scientific productivity, knowledge dissemination, and  interdiscip linary innovation

title: Detecting AI -Generated Text Using Machine Learning
description: With the rapid development of large language models, distinguishing between  human -written and AI -generated text has become increasingly important. This  project aims to develop a machine learning system that can classify whether a piece  of text is written by a human or generated by an AI model. The student will collect  or construct datasets containing both types of text, extract linguistic or statistical  features, and train classification models such as logistic regression, random forests,  or tra nsformer -based classifiers. The project will evaluate the effectiveness of  different features and models and discuss the challenges of detection as AI - generated text becomes more sophisticated.

title: Tabletop RPG Scenario Generator Using DeepSeek
description: DeepSeek is a cutting -edge AI platform specializing in natural language processing  (NLP), capable of generating  human -like text, understanding context, and adapting  to specific domains. Its advanced language models excel in tasks like dialogue  generation, summarization, and creative writing, making it ideal for applications  requiring dynamic and context -aware outpu ts.    This project uses DeepSeek to create a dynamic scenario generator for tabletop role - playing games (RPGs) like Dungeons & Dragons. The system will produce immersive,  customizable storylines, character dialogues, and plot twists based on player choices.   For example, if players decide to negotiate with a dragon instead of fighting,  DeepSeek generates branching narratives, unique NPC interactions, and  consequences in real time.     Follows are the possible components:   ⚫ Scenario Generation: Fine -tune DeepSeek on RPG rulebooks and existing  campaigns to ensure lore consistency.   ⚫ Dialogue Customization: Allow Game Masters (GMs) to input constraints (e.g.,  “medieval fantasy” or “cyberpunk noir”) for tailored storytelling.   ⚫ Visu alization Tools: Integrate simple APIs (e.g., DALL -E) to generate scene  illustrations alongside text.

title: AI Ethics Analysis and Evaluation
description: Fast development of AI, like ChatGPPT, GPT-4, has put AI ethics at a level with impact to span from traditional NPOs to overall businesses.  In this project, you are expected to conduct domain relevant (including information cocoon, AI intimacy) analysis of AI Ethics Analysis and Evaluation.

title: Sustainable LLMs Development Analysis
description: China has been one of the major players in LLMs. However this status quo is threaten by the de-linking and other disruptions. In this context, to sustain China’s LLMs further development capacity, in this project, you are expected to conduct China’s LLMs Development Analysis to identify a sustainable path.

title: AlphaFold3: Predicting Pharmaceuticals Potential via Protein Interaction Analysis
description: AlphaFold 3.0 provides open source code to predict the 3-D structure of arbitrary proteins with unprecedented accuracy.  AlphaFold v2.0 is a completely new model that was entered in the CASP14 assessment and published in Nature (Jumper et al. 2021). The package contains source code, trained weights, and an inference script. In this project, you are expected to leverage the capacity of AlphaFold 3.0 to predict Protein structures and interactions with intention of Pharmaceuticals potentials.

title: AI and Security Automation for Red, Blue, and Purple Teams
description: Availability of AI systems like ChatGPPT, GPT-4 is leading to a revolutionary era for AI to yield unforeseen impact to the world. In this project, you are expect to leverage LLMs in the context of AI and Security Automation for Red, Blue, and Purple Teams.

title: Software Engineering for Agentic AI
description: Availability of AI systems like ChatGPPT, GPT-4 is leading to a revolutionary era for AI to yield unforeseen impact to the world. Future impact of AI heavily rests upon AI Engineering, an emergent discipline focused on developing tools, systems, and processes to enable the application of artificial intelligence in real-world contexts. In this project, you are expect to study software engineering for Agentic AI with a specific industry application.

title: Lee-Oscillator-Enhanced Quantum Price Level (QPL) Model for Chaotic Forex Trading
description: Forex markets exhibit extreme volatility and chaotic behavior driven by macroeconomic news, interest rate differentials, geopolitical events, and herd trading effects. This project integrates Lee-oscillators’ transient chaotic dynamics into the Quantum Price Level (QPL) framework to model nonlinear exchange rate thresholds and risk boundaries more realistically. Students will design a chaotic neural network that processes high-frequency tick or daily data for major currency pairs (e.g., EUR/USD, USD/JPY, GBP/USD), fusing QPL-derived quantum signals with oscillator-based retrograde feedback to forecast Value-at-Risk (VaR) or expected shortfall. The system aims to reduce prediction errors (MAE/RMSE) compared to traditional LSTM/GRU models, offering more robust trading signals in highly uncertain forex environments. Evaluation will use real-world datasets (e.g., from 2015–2025) with backtesting under realistic spreads and slippage.

title: Chaotic Neural Oscillatory Deep Reinforcement Learning for Quantum Finance-Inspired Portfolio Optimization
description: Traditional portfolio optimization struggles with non-stationary market regimes and abrupt regime shifts. Building upon LORSTransformerDRL concepts, this project develops an end-to-end deep reinforcement learning agent that replaces standard neurons with Lee-oscillator units to capture transient chaos in asset returns. The agent directly learns optimal buy/hold/sell policies by incorporating quantum finance signals (QPL thresholds) as part of the state representation. Students will implement the model, train it on multi-asset time-series (indices, forex, commodities), and evaluate risk-adjusted performance (Sharpe ratio, maximum drawdown, cumulative return) under realistic transaction costs and slippage. The goal is to achieve superior out-of-sample generalization in volatile global markets.

title: Transient-Chaotic Type-2 Fuzzy Neural Networks with Lee-Oscillators for Worldwide Commodity Price Forecasting
description: Commodity prices are influenced by geopolitical events, supply shocks, and nonlinear feedback loops, making accurate long-term forecasting difficult. Extending the CT2TFDNN architecture, this project embeds Lee-oscillators into a type-2 fuzzy deep neuro-oscillatory network to handle uncertainty via chaotic transient-fuzzy membership functions and retrograde signaling. Students will fuse top financial signals (technical indicators + quantum-derived features) and train the model on historical data of major commodities (oil, gold, metals, agriculture). The system targets reduced forecasting errors (RMSE, MAPE) and better handling of abrupt price jumps compared to classical recurrent networks. Real-world backtesting and ablation studies will validate chaotic components’ contribution to robustness.

title: Quantum Finance Sentiment Analysis Using Chaotic Dual-Feature Neural Oscillators for Stock Volatility Prediction
description: Investor sentiment from social media and news significantly drives short-term stock volatility, yet most models fail to capture bidirectional chaotic dependencies. This project combines Lee-oscillators with a dual-feature chaotic neural architecture (inspired by CDF-GCN principles) to jointly process textual sentiment embeddings and quantum price level thresholds. Students will build an end-to-end system that predicts intraday/next-day volatility for selected stocks, using chaotic weight oscillation to model nonlinear sentiment-market interactions. Performance will be benchmarked against standard GARCH, LSTM, and transformer baselines using metrics such as directional accuracy, MAE, and calibration scores. The work aims to demonstrate improved early warning of volatility spikes in sentiment-driven regimes.

title: CNO-Former Extension: Chaotic Neural Oscillatory Transformers for Advanced Social Media Dialogue Generation
description: Social media conversations are highly dynamic, context-dependent, and often exhibit abrupt topic shifts. This project extends the CNO-former by further refining chaotic dot-product attention with Lee-oscillator retrograde signaling to better preserve long-range dependencies while reducing quadratic complexity. Students will fine-tune the model on large-scale dialogue datasets (e.g., Reddit, Twitter threads) for open-domain response generation. The chaotic mechanism aims to improve coherence, diversity, and emotional expressiveness, evaluated via BLEU, ROUGE, Dist-n, PPL, and human preference scores. Comparisons against vanilla transformers, Performer, and Reformer will highlight gains in information retention and convergence speed for real-time chat applications.

title: Chaotic Lee-Oscillator Transformers for Multilingual Text Summarization with Nonlinear Sentiment Handling
description: Multilingual news and reviews contain sentiment fluctuations and cultural nuances that standard transformers struggle to model effectively. This project infuses Lee-oscillators into the transformer encoder-decoder to introduce transient chaotic dynamics in self-attention layers, enabling better capture of nonlinear sentiment transitions across languages. Students will train and evaluate abstractive summarization models on multilingual datasets (e.g., MLSum, XL-Sum). The chaotic component targets higher ROUGE-L and BERTScore while reducing exposure bias and repetition. Ablation experiments will quantify the contribution of oscillatory behavior to handling low-resource languages and emotionally charged content.

title: Transient-Chaotic Neural Networks with Lee-Oscillators for Machine Translation in Noisy NLP Environments
description: User-generated content (social media, forums) is noisy, containing slang, typos, emojis, and code-switching, challenging conventional seq2seq models. Drawing inspiration from TCAN’s progressive memory recalling, this project replaces standard activation functions in transformer translation layers with Lee-oscillator chaotic units to simulate dynamic, adaptive memory association under noise. Students will build and fine-tune models for noisy→clean translation pairs in high-resource and low-resource language pairs. Evaluation focuses on BLEU, chrF, COMET, and robustness metrics. The chaotic approach is expected to yield more fluent and contextually accurate translations in real-world noisy settings.

title: Lee-Oscillator-Infused Chaotic Transformers for NLP-Based Fake News Detection and Progressive Fact-Checking
description: Fake news spreads rapidly via progressive narrative escalation and subtle linguistic manipulation. This project integrates Lee-oscillators into transformer architectures to enable chaotic progressive pattern association for detecting evolving misinformation. Students will design a model that processes claim + evidence pairs or threaded social media posts, using oscillatory dynamics to model uncertainty propagation over time. Training uses datasets like FakeNewsNet, LIAR, or FEVER; evaluation includes F1, AUC, explainability via attention visualization, and progressive accuracy improvement. The chaotic mechanism aims to outperform static BERT/RoBERTa baselines, especially on emerging or evolving false narratives.

title: Multi-Agent Chaotic Neural Collaboration System with Lee-Oscillators for Dynamic Office Resource Allocation
description: Hybrid offices suffer from meeting room overuse/underuse, booking conflicts, and last-minute changes. Extending OfficeMind, this project equips autonomous agents (representing users/resources) with Lee-oscillator chaotic units to perform nonlinear priority negotiation and conflict resolution. Chaotic dynamics enable agents to escape local optima during real-time bidding and adapt to sudden demand surges. Students will implement a decentralized MAS with master-slave coordination, integrate with calendar APIs (Outlook/Slack), and simulate/evaluate in 10–50 room scenarios. Targets include ≥25% utilization increase, ≥30% conflict reduction, and faster convergence (<3s) compared to rule-based or conventional RL agents.

title: Lee-Oscillator-Infused Chaotic Transformers for Narrative Consistency Analysis in Criminal Testimony
description: Criminal testimony is characterized by inherent non-linear dynamics—such as cognitive hesitation, memory reconstruction, and temporal inconsistencies—which conventional static NLP architectures (e.g., BERT, RoBERTa) fail to model effectively. This project proposes an innovative integration of Lee-oscillators into Transformer-based frameworks to simulate the chaotic evolution of narrative structures in legal statements. By leveraging the chaotic bifurcation and strange attractor properties of Lee-oscillators, the model aims to capture the underlying cognitive fluctuations during testimony. Students will develop a system capable of processing sequential transcripts for high-stakes tasks, including reliability quantification, latent contradiction detection, and multi-turn consistency analysis. Benchmarked against state-of-the-art baselines, this chaotic mechanism seeks to achieve superior performance in identifying the complex, dynamic patterns of human memory and deception.

title: Underwater Robot with Buoy Relay System
description: This project aims to design and implement a buoy-based relay communication system for underwater robots. Underwater robots face challenges in real-time data transmission due to the limitations of acoustic communication. By utilizing surface buoys as relay stations, we can establish a hybrid communication link (acoustic from robot to buoy, and radio from buoy to shore) to improve bandwidth and reliability. The student will develop a communication protocol, simulate the system, and possibly implement a prototype using low-cost hardware (e.g., Arduino, Raspberry Pi) to demonstrate data relay and robot control. Machine learning techniques may be explored to predict channel conditions and optimize transmission. The project will evaluate performance metrics such as latency, packet loss, and energy efficiency.

title: Desktop Robot Based on Large Language Models
description: This project focuses on developing an interactive desktop robot powered by large language models (LLMs). The robot will be capable of understanding and generating natural language, enabling seamless human-robot interaction. The student will integrate an LLM (such as GPT) with a robotic platform (e.g., a simple robotic arm or mobile base) to perform tasks based on user commands. Key components include speech recognition, intent detection, dialogue management, and low-level robot control. The project will explore prompt engineering, fine-tuning of LLMs for robotics, and real-time response optimization. Evaluation will be based on task completion accuracy, user satisfaction, and response latency.

title: Automatic Cleaning Device for Underwater Cameras
description: Underwater cameras are prone to biofouling and debris accumulation, degrading image quality. This project aims to develop an automatic cleaning system for underwater camera lenses. The system will use computer vision techniques to detect dirt or blurriness in the captured images, triggering a mechanical cleaning mechanism (e.g., wiper, water jet, or ultrasonic cleaner). The student will design and implement an image quality assessment algorithm, integrate it with a microcontroller (e.g., Arduino, Raspberry Pi), and test the prototype in a controlled water environment. The project may also explore machine learning for fouling prediction and adaptive cleaning schedules. Performance will be evaluated based on image clarity restoration and system reliability.

title: Open Project
description: This open topic project encourages students to propose and develop innovative solutions to real-life problems using embedded systems and AI algorithms. Students are expected to select a suitable microcontroller or single-board computer (e.g., ESP32, STM32, Raspberry Pi) and integrate various sensors (such as cameras, microphones, temperature, humidity, motion sensors, etc.) to build a functional prototype. The project should combine hardware with intelligent algorithms (e.g., machine learning, computer vision, signal processing) to create a practical application that addresses everyday challenges, such as smart home automation, health monitoring, environmental sensing, assistive devices for the elderly or disabled, or any other context where embedded AI can make a difference. The outcome should be a working system demonstrating the integration of hardware and software to solve a tangible problem. Students will document their design, implementation, and testing process, and present the final prototype.

title: Artificial expansion of digital images
description: Description  Digital images are in our daily life from cell phone to professional camera. Expansion of digital images encounters challenging issues of zigzag artifacts. This project is to find automatic and artificial expansion methods to make the expanded images without zigzag artifacts in comparison with traditional image expansion methods.  Digital images are in our daily life from cell phone to professional camera. Expansion of digital images encounters challenging issues of zigzag artifacts. This project is to find automatic and artificial expansion methods to make the expanded images without zigzag artifacts in comparison with traditional image expansion methods.

title: Application of data mining to image edge detection and enhancement
description: Data mining has many mining methods in extracting useful information in data distribution. This project is to investigate the data mining methods for image edge detection and image enhancement.

title: Deep learning method for medical image classification
description: Medical image classification has wide applications in disease diagnosis and treatment. This project is to apply deep learning model to medical image classification such as the tumor detection and classification of different images such as X-ray, CT images or MRI images to improve predication rate of correct diseases from medical images.

title: Image fusion based medical image processing
description: Description  Image fusion-based medical image processing is a technique that combines information from multiple imaging modalities to create a single, comprehensive image that enhances the visualization and analysis of anatomical structures and pathologies. This approach leverages the strengths of different imaging techniques, such as MRI, CT, PET, and ultrasound, which each provide unique insights into various aspects of tissue composition, function, and structure. By fusing images, clinicians can obtain a more detailed and accurate representation of the area of interest, facilitating better diagnosis, treatment planning, and monitoring of disease progression. Image fusion helps overcome the limitations of individual modalities, such as MRI's excellent soft tissue contrast and CT's superior bone imaging, by integrating these complementary data sets into a unified image that improves clinical decision-making and patient outcomes.

title: LLM-Guided Distilled Feedback for Universal Segmentation Enhancement
description: We propose a novel framework for segmentation enhancement that leverages large language and vision-language models (LLM/VLM) as perceptual judges to guide model optimization. Candidate mask perturbations are generated for segmentation outputs, which are evaluated by LLMs to produce ranking signals and natural language feedback. These signals are then distilled into a differentiable evaluator network, allowing end-to-end fine-tuning of any segmentation model. Our approach enables weakly-supervised, perceptually-informed segmentation optimization, improving semantic consistency, edge fidelity, and robustness across multiple segmentation tasks.

title: Archery Score Reporting system
description: An archery score reporting system is a comprehensive digital platform designed to streamline the process of recording and analyzing scores during archery competitions. This system typically includes features such as real-time score updates, automated calculations, and detailed statistical analysis, enabling participants and organizers to track performance efficiently. The platform can be accessed via mobile devices or computers, allowing archers to input their scores directly after each round, while judges and officials can verify and validate the results. Additionally, the system often provides visual representations of scoring trends and historical data, helping archers to identify areas for improvement and strategize for future competitions. Overall, the archery score reporting system enhances the accuracy, transparency, and accessibility of score management in the sport.

title: Neural Tone Transform
description: By importing pre-trained deep learning models, it instantly converts input signals from ordinary instruments (such as low-cost electric guitars or electronic keyboards) into high-fidelity target instrument tones (including pre-trained premium orchestral instruments, classic synthesizers, or specific cabinet emulations). While maintaining ultra-low latency during performance, it can accurately preserve the dynamic expression and playing nuances of the original input signal.
